message:
fcp-e2e: google-docs + google-sheets compliance harnesses

## Why

The google-drive connector got its FCP2 manifest + shared compliance
harness in 6653c5d0, which gave google-drive full ComplianceSuite
coverage. The sibling google-workspace connectors — google-docs and
google-sheets — were still running with ad-hoc per-connector tests and
had no parity with the harness-driven suite google-drive now uses.

These two new E2E tests close that gap. Each wires its connector
(DocsConnector, SheetsConnector) through the shared E2eRunner +
ComplianceSuite + ConnectorSuite surface and exercises the same three
invariants that every harness-driven connector must honor:

  1. Default-deny: a request with no (or wrong) capability token is
     rejected before the connector touches the wire.
  2. Allow path: a capability token issued by a trusted Ed25519 signer
     for the matching CapabilityId + ConnectorId is honored and the
     connector makes the expected outbound call.
  3. Network guard: outbound HTTP is constrained by the manifest's
     network allowlist — mocked hosts outside the manifest are
     rejected; mocked hosts inside the manifest succeed.

## Implementation notes

  - Both tests are feature-gated (`#[cfg(feature = "google_docs")]`
    and `#[cfg(feature = "google_sheets")]`) so workspace builds
    without the corresponding connector feature skip them cleanly.
  - Capability tokens are minted in-test via
    CapabilityTokenBuilder + Ed25519SigningKey; no real Google OAuth
    is exercised — wire responses come from MockApiServer +
    wiremock::Mock.
  - `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` matches the existing
    compliance harness convention; these suites deliberately inline
    all three scenarios per file so the invariants read top-to-bottom
    without cross-file indirection.

No connector source touched; this is test-only coverage expansion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
